title: Detecting Connected Components in a Graph

statement.markdown: You are given a graph represented as an adjacency list. Write a function to detect the number of connected components in the graph. A connected component is a subgraph in which there is a path between any two vertices.
statement.inputFormat: You will be given a graph represented as an adjacency list where each key is a node and its corresponding value is a list of its adjacent nodes.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return the number of connected components in the graph.
statement.notes: You can make use of a visited set to keep track of visited nodes.

examples.explanation: The graph has two connected components, {1, 2, 3, 4} and {5, 6, 7}

editorial.title: Detecting Connected Components in a Graph
editorial.intuition: Use DFS to traverse the graph and count the number of connected components.
editorial.optimalSolution: Start DFS traversal from an arbitrary node. When you traverse a new node that has not been visited before, increment the count of connected components.
